_publications._publicationTitle: Programming of Schwann Cells by Lats1/2-TAZ/YAP Signaling Drives Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumorigenesis.

_contextSnippet: Among the GFP+ tumor cells, approximately 55.3 ± 2.8% expressed both Sox10 and the stem-cell/progenitor marker Sox2...a fraction of GFP+ cells (15.5 ± 5.2%) were Sox2+ Sox10−...the majority of GFP+ cells (81.5 ± 6.4%) expressed nuclear TAZ/YAP
details: Among GFP+ tumor cells, approximately 55.3 ± 2.8% expressed both Sox10 and Sox2, and 15.5 ± 5.2% were Sox2+ Sox10−, with 81.5 ± 6.4% expressing nuclear TAZ/YAP
